From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Football tournament season

1975–76 Cupa României

| Tournament details | |
| --- | --- |
| Country | Romania |
| Final positions | |
| Champions | [Steaua București](/wiki/FC_Steaua_Bucure%C8%99ti "FC Steaua București") |
| Runners-up | [CSU Galaţi](/wiki/CSU_Dun%C4%83rea_de_Jos_Gala%C8%9Bi "CSU Dunărea de Jos Galați") |
| ← [1974–75](/wiki/1974%E2%80%9375_Cupa_Rom%C3%A2niei "1974–75 Cupa României")  [1976–77](/wiki/1976%E2%80%9377_Cupa_Rom%C3%A2niei "1976–77 Cupa României") → | |

The **1975–76 [Cupa României](/wiki/Cupa_Rom%C3%A2niei "Cupa României")** was the 38th edition of [Romania](/wiki/Romania "Romania")'s most prestigious [football](/wiki/Association_football "Association football") [cup competition](/wiki/Single-elimination_tournament "Single-elimination tournament").

The title was won by [Steaua București](/wiki/FC_Steaua_Bucure%C8%99ti "FC Steaua București") against [CSU Galaţi](/wiki/CSU_Dun%C4%83rea_de_Jos_Gala%C8%9Bi "CSU Dunărea de Jos Galați").

## Format

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=1975%E2%80%9376_Cupa_Rom%C3%A2niei&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Format")]

The competition is an annual [knockout](/wiki/Single-elimination_tournament "Single-elimination tournament") tournament.

First round proper matches are played on the ground of the lowest ranked team, then from the second round proper the matches are played on a neutral location.

If a match is drawn after 90 minutes, the game goes in [extra time](/wiki/Overtime_(sports) "Overtime (sports)"), and if the scored is still tight after 120 minutes, then the winner will be established at [penalty kicks](/wiki/Penalty_shoot-out_(association_football) "Penalty shoot-out (association football)").

From the first edition, the teams from [Divizia A](/wiki/Divizia_A "Divizia A") entered in competition in sixteen finals, rule which remained till today.[[1]](#cite_note-ripensia-sport-magazin-1)
